reasoning: Current TSLA price as of late July/August 2025 is around $316. While TSLA experienced a massive ~50% correction earlier in the year (from roughly $480 in Dec 2024 to ~$240 in Q1 2025), it did not breach the $200 mark. To hit <$200 before the end of 2025, the stock needs to decline by ~37% from its current level over the next ~5 months. Given TSLA's immense historically high annualized volatility (around 65%), a 37% plunge in 5 months is very plausible. Using standard structural models for first-passage time (hitting a lower barrier) with these parameters yields a theoretical probability of roughly 25-28% of touching the $200 barrier before year-end. This aligns almost perfectly with the Manifold market estimate of 26% on July 24th. With no major fundamental changes to deviate from the crowd's efficiently priced estimate, 25% represents the most finely tuned and modeled probability.
